title: How an Accidental Discovery in a Texas Lab Could Reshape Battery Manufacturing
summary: A team of scientists at Texas A&M University accidentally discovered a new method for producing a critical component in lithium-ion batteries. The team, based in College Station, Texas, was trying to study an entirely different phenomenon when they discovered that they could produce graphene oxide …

title: Global Nuclear Power Capacity Set to Jump by 44% by 2036
summary: The world’s fleet of nuclear power capacity is expected to surge by 44% over the next decade as China will topple the United States as the biggest nuclear power capacity holder and India will hike its capacity to boost energy security. These are the estimates in a new report by BloombergNEF, which …

title: EIA: Crude Oil Inventories in US See Rare Build
summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States saw a surprise increase of 3.0 million barrels during the week ending July 3, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. title: Vitol Eyes Venezuela Expansion as Oil Industry Returns
summary: Commodity trading major Vitol is planning to establish a presence in Venezuela amid a surge in interest in the country’s oil wealth from the resources industry, Reuters has reported, citing unnamed sources. title: The maths on London’s IPO drought
summary: The value of bids for companies on the London Stock Exchange is 27 times greater than the value of new entrants this year
